x-enumeration-literals.name: Fixed Amount Deduction
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Redemption consumes a predefined fixed value of rewards.

Comment:
Example: Redeem 500 points to pay for a $10 item.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Percentage-Based Deduction
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Deduction is a percentage of the transaction value or payable amount.

Comment:
Example: Use 10% of your total bill via points.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Tiered Deduction
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Deduction rate varies based on transaction amount or points applied.

Comment:
Example: 100 points per $10 for purchases up to $100, 90 points per $10 above $100.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Conditional Deduction
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Points are deducted only if specific conditions are met.

Comment:
Example: Redeem points only if transaction amount exceeds $50.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Dynamic Deduction
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Deduction is calculated using a custom formula involving transaction amount, points balance, or multipliers.

Comment:
Example: Points consumed = (Transaction Amount × Conversion Rate) − Discounts.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Full or Partial Redemption
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Rules specify whether all points or only part of the points are burned.

Comment:
Example: Option to pay fully with points or partially with points and cash.
